items.title_original: Why 70% of Manufacturing ERP Projects Miss ROI Targets
items.title_neutral: Why 70% of Manufacturing ERP Projects Miss ROI Targets
items.excerpt: Manufacturing ERP projects rarely fail because of software. Most fail due to unclear goals, inefficient processes, poor data quality, and weak user adoption. Organizations that treat ERP as a business transformation, not an IT project, are far more likely to achieve measurable ROI.
items.recap_neutral: Manufacturing ERP projects rarely fail because of software
items.main_idea: Why 70% of Manufacturing ERP Projects Miss ROI Targets

items.title_original: Virtual Development Teams Made of AI Agents: Hype or Real Shift in Workflows?
items.title_neutral: Virtual Development Teams Made of AI Agents: Hype or Real Shift in Workflows?
items.excerpt: I see a lot of posts along the lines of AI saved us a ton of money, but they almost never include actual numbers or explain how those numbers were derived. So I decided to break down my approach and be honest about where it gets shaky, because like 10x improvement means nothing without a baseline.
items.recap_neutral: I see a lot of posts along the lines of AI saved us a ton of money, but they almost never include actual numbers or explain how those numbers were derived
items.main_idea: Virtual Development Teams Made of AI Agents: Hype or Real Shift in Workflows?
